title: gRPC streaming for exec stdout/stderr
description: Use tonic server-side streaming to stream exec stdout/stderr as raw bytes (no base64). Implement buffered streaming: guest agent buffers output to 64KB before sending (reduces framing overhead ~16x for chatty processes). On host side, expose as AsyncRead/tokio stream. This replaces both the base64 encoding overhead (33% bloat) and the small-chunk framing problem.
notes: parent: vz-bdm
close_reason: Increased guest-side exec stream buffer from 8KB to 64KB (16x fewer frames for chatty processes). Added GrpcExecStream wrapper on host side mirroring legacy ExecStream API (next/collect). Binary data uses native protobuf bytes (no base64 overhead).

title: Lightweight container-namespace isolation mode
description: Current IsolationMode::RestoreOnAcquire costs 5-10s per session acquisition (full VM state restore). Add a new isolation tier that uses Linux container namespaces (pid, mount, net, user) inside a single running VM to provide session isolation without VM restart. Target: sub-second session acquisition with reasonable isolation guarantees. Keep RestoreOnAcquire for strict security requirements.
close_reason: Wave 6 implementation complete

title: ServiceSpec → RunConfig converter

close_reason: ServiceSpec→RunConfig converter with 20 tests, all passing

title: vz boot progress lacks polish — no spinner, timing confusing
description:
When running `vz`, the boot experience feels rough:
1. 'Booting sandbox vz-xxxx...' then nothing for 1-2 seconds
2. 'waiting for guest agent (attempt 5: ...)' shows error details users don't need to see
3. 'Sandbox ready (1.8s)' then drops into shell with no visual separation

Should have:
- Animated spinner during boot
- Clean progress without raw error details
- Clear transition from boot to shell (e.g., blank line or separator)
- Consider showing sandbox info on attach (name, dir, resources)

title: Docker CLI command shim: translate docker run/exec/ps/logs/stop/rm to vz primitives
description:
runtime-v2/07-docker-compose-compatibility-shims.md defines a Docker command shim that maps high-value Docker CLI commands to vz runtime operations. This is separate from Compose shim (vz-20i covers Compose).

V1 command set:
- docker run → create/start ephemeral container (with --rm auto-remove)
- docker exec → exec_container on running container
- docker ps → list running containers
- docker logs → fetch/stream container logs
- docker pull → resolve image by digest
- docker build → start_build + stream events
- docker stop → stop container
- docker rm → remove container

Implementation:
- New module in vz-cli/src/commands/docker.rs (or docker_shim.rs).
- Map each Docker command to existing runtime operations.
- Strict flag mapping table: supported flags map directly, unsupported flags return unsupported_operation with exact flag name and reason.
- Do not emulate Docker daemon — this is CLI translation only.

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/07-docker-compose-compatibility-shims.md
close_reason: Wave 6 implementation complete

title: Epic: Host Port Forwarding for Stacks
description: Host-side TCP listeners start but guest agent always connects to 127.0.0.1:target_port. In stack mode, containers run in per-service netns so 127.0.0.1 can't reach them. Need to add target_host to PortForward protocol so connections route to service IPs (172.20.0.x).
close_reason: All dependencies completed: target_host protocol, executor wiring, and E2E test all done.

title: vz ls output is ugly — no color, poor alignment, no time-ago format
description:
The `vz ls` output is a raw table with fixed-width columns. Issues:
1. No color highlighting for state (running=green, failed=red)
2. Timestamps show raw unix epoch, not relative time ('2m ago', '1h ago')
3. No headers differentiation (bold/underline)
4. DIR column shows full absolute path, should use ~ shorthand
5. Should show 'No sandboxes.' in a friendly way when empty, not just bare text

Compare with `docker ps` or `kubectl get pods` for reference.

title: Implement Execution as tracked domain entity with lifecycle states
description:
runtime-v2/01-domain-model.md defines Execution as a standalone entity for interactive or batch command execution inside a container, with states: queued → running → exited | failed | canceled.

Currently exec is fire-and-forget — there is no Execution object, no state tracking, no cancel/resize support.

Implementation:
- Add Execution struct: id, container_id, spec (cmd, args, env, pty, timeout), state, exit_code, created_at.
- Add ExecutionState enum: Queued, Running, Exited, Failed, Canceled.
- Track active executions per container in state store.
- Support cancel_exec (signal running execution) and resize_exec_pty.
- Emit ExecutionQueued, ExecutionRunning, ExecutionExited, ExecutionFailed, ExecutionCanceled events.
- Expose via vz-api /v1/executions endpoints.
- This is prerequisite for vz-pqs (exec resize/cancel primitives).

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/01-domain-model.md
close_reason: Closed

title: Executor: deduplicate pull + serialize rootfs assembly for same-image replicas
description:
When the executor creates multiple replicas of the same service, all replicas share the same image. Currently each replica thread independently calls pull() and then create_in_sandbox(), leading to concurrent rootfs assembly of the same image layers. This causes race conditions (layer extraction corruption, 'No such file or directory' errors) when multiple replicas of the same image are created in parallel.

Fix: pull the image once before entering the parallel thread::scope, and serialize rootfs assembly for containers sharing the same image (or make the runtime's rootfs assembly thread-safe). This is needed for replica support to work reliably in the executor.
close_reason: Deduplicated image pulls in executor: pull each unique image once serially before parallel create_in_sandbox. Also improved runtime overlay cleanup for recreate scenarios.

title: BuildKit Integration — docker build for vz
description:
Enable vz build to build OCI images from Dockerfiles, powered by BuildKit running inside a lightweight Linux guest VM. Full docker build compatibility (multi-stage, cache mounts, secrets) without Docker Desktop.

Architecture: buildkitd runs in a dedicated Linux VM, host communicates over vsock gRPC proxy. BuildKit's session protocol tunnels build context, auth, and file exports through a single connection. Static arm64 binaries (~84 MB) provisioned like youki.

## Success Criteria
- vz build . builds a Dockerfile end-to-end
- vz build -t myapp:latest . tags and imports into vz store
- vz build --push pushes to registry
- vz run myapp:latest runs the built image
- Persistent build cache survives VM restarts
- BuildKit VM lazy-boots and auto-shuts down on idle

## Key Decisions
- Single vsock port for all gRPC (build, context, auth, progress)
- Static binary provisioning (buildkitd + buildkit-runc from GitHub releases)
- Rust gRPC client via buildkit-client crate or bollard-buildkit-proto
- Persistent cache via VirtioFS mount (~/.vz/buildkit/cache/)
- Dedicated VM (not shared with container stacks)

## Plan Reference
- planning/buildkit/README.md
- planning/buildkit/01-artifact-provisioning.md through 06-cache-management.md
notes: BuildKit functionality is now validated end-to-end with signed harness (./scripts/run-buildkit-e2e.sh): build from Dockerfile with RUN, import into local store, and runtime execution all pass. Architecture delivered via direct in-guest buildctl orchestration (superseding planned vsock gRPC client/proxy phases). Follow-up for persistent cache on direct VirtioFS root tracked in vz-2xv.

title: (EPIC) Docker Compose network parity — shared VM with per-service netns isolation
description: Replace the current 1-VM-per-service model with a shared-VM-per-stack model where all services run as youki containers inside a single Linux VM. Each service gets its own network namespace connected via a veth bridge, enabling proper inter-service communication by name with real IPs. This achieves Docker Compose networking semantics: DNS/hosts-based service discovery, network isolation between services, same-port binding without conflicts, and correct port mapping to host.

title: Add guest agent network setup protocol (bridge, netns, veth, IP)
description: Extend the vz-linux guest agent with a new NetworkSetup capability and request type. The agent needs to perform these operations inside the VM: (1) Create a bridge interface (br0) for a stack, (2) Create a named network namespace per service, (3) Create a veth pair per service, move one end into the service netns, attach the other to the bridge, (4) Assign a deterministic IP address inside each netns (e.g., 172.20.0.<n>/24), (5) Bring up all interfaces and set default routes. IMPORTANT: busybox ip does NOT support ip netns or veth/bridge creation. The agent must use Rust netlink (rtnetlink crate or nix) for namespace creation (unshare/setns syscalls), bridge creation (RTM_NEWLINK), veth pair creation (RTM_NEWLINK with IFLA_INFO_KIND=veth), IP assignment (RTM_NEWADDR), and route setup (RTM_NEWROUTE). This keeps everything self-contained with no external tool dependencies. The protocol is JSON-over-vsock frames. Follow the existing OCI lifecycle dispatch pattern in agent.rs.
close_reason: Added NetworkSetup/NetworkTeardown protocol types, host-side LinuxVm methods, guest agent handlers with raw netlink network module. All 680 tests pass.

The ContainerRuntime trait in executor.rs may need a new method like create_in_vm() or the existing create() needs to accept an optional VM handle. Key constraint: existing single-container create_container() must continue working for non-stack use cases.

This tells youki to join an existing network namespace (created by the agent) instead of using the default. Uses the oci_spec::runtime crate's LinuxBuilder and LinuxNamespaceBuilder. Also needs to emit linux.devices if required by youki for namespace-aware containers.

title: [vz-stack] Secrets are mounted as directories instead of files in shared VM mode
description:
When using file-based secrets in a multi-service stack (which uses shared VM mode), the secret is mounted as a directory instead of a file.

## Steps to Reproduce
```yaml
services:
  db:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    secrets:
      - db_password
secrets:
  db_password:
    file: ./secret.txt
```

## Expected
- Secret file content should be at /run/secrets/db_password as a FILE

## Actual
- /run/secrets/db_password is a DIRECTORY

## Debug Info
- Host file exists: ~/.vz/stacks/<name>/secrets/<name>/db_password (is a FILE with correct content)
- Container mount shows: /run/secrets/db_password is a directory (drwxr-xr-x)

## Suspected Root Cause
The mount path construction might be pointing to the parent directory (secrets/) instead of the specific file (secrets/db_password)

Location to check: secrets_to_mounts() in vz-stack/src/convert.rs or how it's used in shared VM mode

title: cd /workspace sent before raw mode hides prompt
description:
In attach_interactive(), the `cd /workspace\n` command is sent before raw mode is enabled and before the output reading loop starts. This means:
1. The shell prompt after cd might appear before we're reading output, getting lost
2. The user sees a blank screen initially because the prompt was consumed before the output loop started

Fix: Either send the cd command via the PTY working_dir (set working_dir on the ExecRequest), or delay the cd until after the output loop is running.

title: Expose checkpoint create/restore/fork as first-class Runtime V2 API primitives
description:
Expose checkpoint create/restore/fork as first-class Runtime V2 API primitives with two checkpoint classes.

runtime-v2/04-snapshot-checkpoint-replay.md defines:
- fs_quick: filesystem-focused checkpoint (writable layers + metadata). Baseline for all backends.
- vm_full: full machine state (RAM, CPU, devices, disk). Capability-gated, only where backend supports.

Current state: vz::Vm::save_state()/restore_state() exists for macOS VM full state, but there is no Checkpoint domain entity, no fs_quick class, no API surface, no lineage tracking.

Implementation:
- Checkpoint domain entity with id, class (FsQuick|VmFull), sandbox_id, lineage, compatibility_fingerprint, state (Creating|Ready|Failed), created_at.
- create_checkpoint flow: validate lease → optional guest quiesce hook → pause → capture by class → persist metadata → emit events → receipt.
- restore_checkpoint flow: validate fingerprint → materialize layers → restore by class → rotate lease/endpoints → resume health checks.
- fork_checkpoint: branch lineage, new sandbox gets new IDs, copy-on-write where supported.
- API: POST /v1/checkpoints, POST /v1/checkpoints/{id}/restore, POST /v1/checkpoints/{id}/fork.
- Capability gate: vm_full only when backend reports vm_full_checkpoint capability.

Blocked by vz-3hx (compatibility fingerprint + lineage tracking).

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/04-snapshot-checkpoint-replay.md

title: Linux Native Host Support
description:
Enable vz to run sandbox/container workloads on Linux hosts while keeping the macOS Virtualization.framework path intact. Introduces backend abstraction with a shared runtime contract so both macOS and Linux backends implement the same trait.

## Success Criteria
- Linux host can run `vz oci run ubuntu:24.04 -- echo ok` successfully
- `vz stack up` works on Linux for supported Compose subset
- Existing macOS tests continue to pass with no functional regression
- Backend selection is explicit and test-covered

- New `vz-runtime-contract` crate: backend traits + backend-neutral runtime types
- New `vz-linux-native` crate: Linux host primitives and lifecycle executor
- macOS adapter wrapping existing vz-oci runtime in `MacosRuntimeBackend`
- `vz-oci::Runtime` becomes facade delegating to `Arc<dyn RuntimeBackend>`

title: [vz-stack] UX: Improve 'vz stack ls' status display with icons and error summaries
description:
The current 'vz stack ls' output shows confusing status like 'partial (0/1)' that users don't understand.

## Current Output
```
NAME                 STATUS           SERVICES  
----------------------------------------------
my-stack            partial (0/1)    1         
another-stack       running          2         
failed-stack        partial (0/1)    1  
```

## Problems
- 'partial (0/1)' is unclear - what does it mean?
- No indication of why a stack failed
- Can't distinguish between 'starting' and 'failed'

## Proposed Wireframe

```
vz stack ls

STACK NAME          STATUS        READY/TOTAL    PORTS               AGE
──────────────────  ────────────  ─────────────  ──────────────────  ───
my-app              ● running     2/2            8080->80, 3000      2m
web-api             ◐ starting    1/2            8081->80            10s
payment-service     ○ failed      0/1            -                   5m
```

title: Implement Sandbox as first-class domain entity with lifecycle state machine
description:
runtime-v2/01-domain-model.md defines Sandbox as the top-level isolation boundary with states: creating → ready → draining → terminated | failed.

Currently the shared VM is booted implicitly by StackExecutor but there is no Sandbox domain object, no state machine, no lifecycle tracking, and no persistence.

Implementation:
- Add Sandbox struct in vz-stack (or vz-runtime-contract) with id, state, resource config, network domain ref, created_at, lineage.
- Add SandboxState enum: Creating, Ready, Draining, Terminated, Failed.
- Enforce state transition rules (e.g. only Ready → Draining, Creating → Ready | Failed).
- Persist Sandbox state in StateStore (new sandbox_state table).
- Emit SandboxCreating, SandboxReady, SandboxDraining, SandboxTerminated events.
- Wire StackExecutor::boot_shared_vm to create Sandbox entity and transition to Ready.
- Wire stack teardown to transition Sandbox through Draining → Terminated.
- Expose via vz-api GET /v1/sandboxes endpoint (currently 501 stub).

Invariants from runtime-v2:
- Sandbox ID is immutable after creation.
- VirtioFS mount topology is immutable for lifetime of sandbox.
- terminated is final.

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/01-domain-model.md, 06-backend-architecture.md
close_reason: Sandbox wired end-to-end: SQLite persistence (sandbox_state table), orchestrator lifecycle (ensure_sandbox/transition_ready/teardown), 5 StackEvent variants, API CRUD handlers, CLI commands (list/inspect/terminate), full test coverage

title: Fix youki --root flag ordering and tilde expansion in linux-native backend
description:
Two bugs found during E2E testing of the linux-native backend inside a macOS VM:

1. --root flag passed as subcommand arg instead of global flag. youki (and runc) expect `--root` before the subcommand: `youki --root <dir> create ...` not `youki create ... --root <dir>`.

2. Default data_dir uses `PathBuf::from("~/.vz/linux-native")` but tilde is a shell expansion — Rust's PathBuf does not expand it. Results in literal paths like `~/.vz/linux-native/state` which don't exist.

Fixed in process.rs (all 6 call sites) and config.rs (default data_dir).

title: gRPC guest agent protocol migration
description: Replace the JSON+base64 vsock protocol with gRPC over vsock (tonic + prost). The current protocol in protocol.rs/channel.rs serializes every message as JSON with base64 for binary data and opens a new vsock connection per operation.

title: Implement idempotency key framework for all mutating API operations
description:
runtime-v2/05-transport-contracts.md requires all mutating create/start operations to accept idempotency keys (Idempotency-Key header for HTTP, idempotency_key field for gRPC).

Currently no idempotency key support exists anywhere in the codebase. This is a cross-cutting concern that affects every mutating endpoint.

Implementation:
- Add IdempotencyKey type to vz-runtime-contract (newtype over String/UUID).
- Add idempotency store in StateStore: (key, operation_type, result_hash, created_at, expires_at).
- Middleware/interceptor for vz-api that checks idempotency store before executing operation.
- On key match + same input → return cached result.
- On key match + different input → return 409 Conflict with state_conflict error code.
- On key miss → execute operation, persist result.
- gRPC equivalent interceptor for control-plane gRPC services.
- Key expiry/cleanup (suggested: 24h TTL).

Operations requiring idempotency keys (from runtime-v2):
- create_sandbox, open_lease, pull_image, start_build, create_container, exec_container, create_checkpoint, fork_checkpoint.

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/05-transport-contracts-grpc-openapi.md
close_reason: Closed

title: Implement orphan resource cleanup on startup recovery
description:
runtime-v2/03-workspace-and-stack-upgrades.md requires that crash during apply must resume without duplicate side effects, and orphan resources from prior runs must be cleaned up.

Currently if the daemon crashes mid-apply, containers from the partial run may be left running with no reconciliation ownership. On restart, the reconciler resumes the action batch but does not detect or clean up orphaned containers that were created but never registered in observed state.

Implementation:
- On orchestrator startup (before reconcile loop), query runtime for all containers matching stack label/namespace.
- Compare with persisted observed_state.
- Containers in runtime but not in observed_state → orphans → stop and remove.
- Containers in observed_state but not in runtime → stale records → mark as Failed/Stopped.
- Emit OrphanCleanup event with details of cleaned resources.
- Add integration test: crash mid-apply → restart → orphans cleaned → converge correctly.

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/03-workspace-and-stack-upgrades.md
close_reason: Wave 5: testing, perf, cleanup, compose parity

title: Pinned IPSW base matrix and patch bundles
description:
Implement a pinned-base distribution model for macOS VM images so provisioning artifacts stay in sync with specific IPSW builds while preserving reliable system-mode startup semantics. This work introduces strict base fingerprinting, signed file-level patch bundles, and release workflows that allow users to consume validated artifacts without local root requirements.

## Success Criteria
- [ ] A versioned supported-base matrix exists and is enforced by CLI verification paths.
- [ ] Patch bundles are signed, pinned to exact base fingerprints, and fail closed on mismatch.
- [ ] Patch apply is deterministic and idempotent with explicit receipts and verification.
- [ ] Release workflows can publish pinned base artifacts and patch bundles for user consumption.
- [ ] Runtime policy remains system mode by default, with clear behavior for user mode.

## Architecture
- Base identity is pinned by IPSW/build metadata plus derived image/platform fingerprints.
- Distribution uses file-level patch operations (not raw block deltas) for auditability and stability.
- Apply flow requires signature + fingerprint verification before any filesystem mutations.

## Plan Reference
- planning/pinned-ipsw-patches/README.md
- planning/pinned-ipsw-patches/architecture.md

title: BuildKit: Docker Hub pull-rate limits break external Dockerfile compatibility sweeps
description:
During a 52-Dockerfile compatibility sweep (docker/awesome-compose + jupyter/docker-stacks), most failures were Docker Hub 429 rate-limit responses from BuildKit metadata/pull resolution.

Evidence:
- Results: /tmp/vz-github-build-sweep-results-v2.tsv
- Logs: /tmp/vz-github-build-sweep-logs-v2
- Clustered failures: /tmp/vz-github-fail-lines-v2.tsv

Observed error pattern:
- toomanyrequests / 429 Too Many Requests from registry-1.docker.io when resolving image manifests.

Impact:
- Makes large real-world compatibility testing unreliable unless authenticated registry creds are consistently applied.

Suggested direction:
- Ensure  can reliably use Docker credential config for BuildKit pulls in external sweeps.
- Improve surfaced error guidance when pull-rate limits are detected (explicit remediation text).
acceptance_criteria: 1) Running a 50+ Dockerfile sweep with authenticated Docker Hub credentials no longer fails due to unauthenticated 429 pull limits.\n2) When 429 occurs, CLI emits clear actionable remediation guidance (e.g., login/credential options).\n3) Add an integration test path or scripted validation that asserts credential forwarding to BuildKit for pulls.
notes:
Implemented auth forwarding for BuildKit guest builds: generate a temporary Docker config from RuntimeConfig auth (Docker config or basic), mount into BuildKit VM, and wire /root/.docker/config.json before buildctl solve. Added stack up auth flags (--docker-config/--username/--password) and runtime auth propagation for stack pulls. Added unit coverage and validated end-to-end with vz build --docker-config and a stack up/down smoke run.
Retest on 2026-02-24 with auth wiring (results: /tmp/vz-github-build-sweep-results-v3-auth.tsv, logs: /tmp/vz-github-build-sweep-logs-v3-auth) produced 8 pass / 44 fail vs previous 5 pass / 47 fail. Docker Hub 429 category dropped from 34 to 23 but remains significant; all 23 logs still include 'Using registry credentials for BuildKit' while errors indicate unauthenticated token flow. Filed follow-up issue vz-44a for unresolved credential application gap.

title: Add event retention policy and periodic compaction for SQLite event store
description:
The events table in StateStore grows unboundedly. For long-running stacks or daemons, this will degrade query performance and consume disk.

Implementation:
- Add configurable retention policy: max_events (default 10000) and max_age (default 7d).
- Add compact_events() method to StateStore that deletes events beyond retention window.
- Call compact_events() on orchestrator startup and periodically (e.g. every 100 rounds).
- Preserve cursor monotonicity — compacted event IDs are never reused.
- Add index on (stack_name, id) for efficient cursor queries.
- Emit EventsCompacted system event with count of removed events.

title: No Ctrl-C handling in sandbox — should detach, not kill

Docker uses Ctrl-P Ctrl-Q as a detach sequence. We should support a similar escape sequence to detach without killing the shell process.

title: Health check timeout enforcement
description:
HealthPoller executes health check commands via runtime.exec() but never applies a timeout. If a health check command hangs (e.g., curl to unresponsive service), it blocks the poller thread forever, stalling the entire stack.

Fix: wire HealthCheckSpec.timeout_secs into the ExecConfig.timeout field when calling runtime.exec() from the health poller. Default to 30s if not specified.

Files: crates/vz-stack/src/health.rs (poll loop where exec is called)
close_reason: Health check timeout via thread::scope + recv_timeout. Default 30s, configurable via hc.timeout_secs. 1 new test.

title: vz-runtimed: SQLite daemon startup pragmas and single-writer guard
description: Enforce daemon-owned SQLite writer semantics at startup. Requirements: (1) only daemon mutates runtime DB; (2) set PRAGMAs journal_mode=WAL, busy_timeout, foreign_keys=ON on startup connection; (3) add daemon leader/startup lock to prevent dual writers for same DB path; (4) run schema version/migration checks only in daemon startup path.
notes: Reworked startup lock to OS-level file locking (try_lock/unlock) to avoid stale lock-file deadlocks after crash; added lock-release-on-drop test.
close_reason: Implemented daemon SQLite startup pragmas (WAL/busy_timeout/foreign_keys), schema-version startup checks, and robust single-writer startup lock with OS file locking + release tests.

title: vz stack ls — list running stacks
description:
No way to see what stacks are running. Add vz stack ls command that scans state directories or control sockets to list active stacks with their service counts and status.

Files: crates/vz-cli/src/commands/stack.rs (new subcommand)
close_reason: vz stack ls scans ~/.vz/stacks/ dirs, shows name/status/services. Supports --json.

title: vz attach <id> has no running VM registry — can't find VMs
description:
The `vz attach` command needs to connect to a running VM, but VMs are ephemeral in-process objects (Arc<LinuxVm>) that don't persist across CLI invocations.

When the user runs `vz` (which boots a VM), then exits, then runs `vz attach vz-xxxx`, there's no running VM to attach to.

Options:
1. Run VMs as background daemons with a registry (PID file, socket path)
2. Store vsock CID in StateStore so we can reconnect
3. Keep VMs running in the same process using a long-lived daemon
notes: Implemented snapshot-backed attach path in CLI: boot_and_attach now restores sandbox snapshot when present and persists snapshot on detach, so attach no longer depends on an in-memory running-VM registry from previous CLI process. Remaining: macOS VM e2e validation and full daemon-owned runtime/session cutover.

title: Implement Lease entity for time-bounded sandbox access control
description:
runtime-v2/01-domain-model.md defines Lease as a time-bounded access grant to sandbox operations with states: opening → active → expired | closed | failed.

Currently there is no concept of leased access — any caller can operate on any sandbox/container at any time with no expiry or heartbeat.

Implementation:
- Add Lease struct: id, sandbox_id, state, granted_at, expires_at, last_heartbeat.
- Add LeaseState enum: Opening, Active, Expired, Closed, Failed.
- Enforce invariant: new container/execution creation requires active lease.
- Add heartbeat_lease operation to extend lease TTL.
- Add close_lease for graceful release.
- Automatic expiry when heartbeat lapses beyond TTL.
- Persist lease state in StateStore.
- Emit LeaseOpened, LeaseHeartbeat, LeaseExpired, LeaseClosed events.
- Expose via vz-api /v1/leases endpoints.

This is required for multi-tenant safety — without leases, concurrent callers can stomp on each other's sandbox state.

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/01-domain-model.md
close_reason: Closed

title: Compose: expose, stdin_open, tty, interactive mode
description: Parse and apply I/O and port declaration fields: (1) expose — declare ports without publishing to host (documentation/metadata, used by links/networks), (2) stdin_open — keep stdin open (docker run -i equivalent), (3) tty — allocate pseudo-TTY (docker run -t equivalent). stdin_open/tty require PTY allocation in the guest agent exec path, which is a deeper change.
notes:
Reopened: compose parser captures expose/stdin_open/tty, but runtime conversion path does not currently wire these fields into run config/PTY behavior end-to-end.
Linked under vz-20i compose parity umbrella; remains open for end-to-end stdin_open/tty runtime wiring.
Claiming implementation: audit and wire stdin_open/tty/expose from compose spec into runtime config and daemon path where supported.
Implemented first runtime-wiring slice: convert path now emits deterministic OCI annotations for compose expose/stdin_open/tty, and OCI bundle generation now consumes io.vz.compose.tty=true to set process terminal mode. Added tests in vz-stack convert and vz-oci bundle for annotation emission + terminal behavior + invalid annotation rejection.
Closed child vz-kbo.1 after adding expose behavior tests and metadata propagation. Remaining work is stdin_open runtime behavior (vz-kbo.2) and default exec/attach PTY defaults (vz-kbo.3).
Progress slice: vz-oci now maps compose stdin_open=true to OCI process terminal mode (interactive backend compromise) with new bundle tests. Child vz-kbo.2 moved to in_progress. Filed follow-up children vz-kbo.2.1 (daemon exec-stdin integration + unsupported diagnostics) and vz-kbo.3.1 (API-level PTY default/override semantics) to complete remaining acceptance criteria.

title: Implement compose expose semantics without host port publishing
description: Wire compose expose to stack networking behavior and metadata so exposed ports are available for service-to-service routing while avoiding host publish side effects. Ensure explicit ports continue to control host bindings.
acceptance_criteria: 1) expose does not create host bindings by itself. 2) service discovery/network setup receives exposed port metadata. 3) tests verify expose-only vs ports-published behavior.
notes:
Follow-up from tty/annotation slice in vz-kbo.
Implemented expose metadata + no-host-bind behavior coverage: convert tests now assert expose-only services do not create host port bindings, and expose+ports only publish explicitly configured host ports. Exposed ports are carried via deterministic OCI annotation (io.vz.compose.expose) for downstream networking/service-discovery consumers.
close_reason: Expose semantics now validated: no implicit host publish, explicit ports preserved, metadata propagated for networking consumers.

title: Implement stdin_open runtime semantics for compose services
description: Implement real stdin_open behavior beyond metadata: keep init stdin available when requested and ensure daemon/exec attach paths can target containers created with stdin_open=true without dropping input channel.
acceptance_criteria: 1) stdin_open=true affects runtime container I/O behavior, not just annotations. 2) integration tests verify stdin data can be sent to an eligible running workload path. 3) unsupported backend cases return deterministic machine errors.
notes:
Follow-up from tty/annotation slice in vz-kbo.
Implemented first runtime behavior slice: vz-oci now maps compose annotation io.vz.compose.stdin_open=true to OCI process terminal mode (backend compromise) so stdin_open changes container IO behavior rather than remaining metadata-only. Added unit tests in vz-oci bundle for stdin_open terminal behavior and invalid-annotation rejection. Remaining acceptance work: end-to-end stdin write path/integration coverage and deterministic unsupported diagnostics for backends without exec stdin streaming.

title: Compose stdin_open: daemon exec-stdin integration and unsupported diagnostics
description: Implement end-to-end stdin_open behavior through daemon-managed execution sessions: allow stdin writes to running compose workloads created with stdin_open=true, and return deterministic unsupported_operation when backend/session path is unavailable. Add integration tests that write stdin and assert observable process output/exit behavior.
notes: Prerequisite progress landed via vz-yc4i.1: daemon execution session registry exists, StreamExecOutput is session-backed, and WriteExecStdin RPC is available with deterministic unsupported_operation semantics when backend interactive stdin is unavailable. Remaining for this bead: wire real backend interactive exec handles so stdin writes reach compose workloads created with stdin_open=true and produce observable output/exit behavior.

title: Default execution PTY behavior for compose tty services
description: Propagate compose tty intent into daemon execution/session defaults where appropriate (attach/exec pathways), while preserving explicit client overrides. Ensure deterministic behavior for service workloads marked tty=true.
acceptance_criteria: 1) tty=true influences default exec/attach PTY mode. 2) explicit API/CLI PTY flags override defaults predictably. 3) tests cover default and override paths.
notes:
Follow-up to bundle terminal wiring in vz-kbo.
Gap analysis: current Runtime V2 CreateExecutionRequest uses a non-optional bool pty, so daemon cannot distinguish explicit false from inherited default. Filed follow-up child vz-kbo.3.1 to add explicit override semantics and compose tty-driven defaults with precedence tests.
Child vz-kbo.3.1 closed: create_execution now supports explicit pty_mode (inherit/enabled/disabled), daemon defaults inherit mode from compose service tty metadata, and override precedence is covered by integration tests. Remaining scope to validate/implement: attach/session stream pathway should mirror the same tty defaulting semantics where applicable.

title: Compose tty: execution API PTY defaulting with explicit override semantics
description: Add first-class PTY defaulting for compose tty services in daemon execution paths. Introduce API contract support for explicit PTY override vs inherit-default semantics, then default create_execution/attach behavior from compose tty metadata while preserving explicit caller overrides. Add tests for default=true, default=false, and explicit override precedence.
close_reason: Implemented PTY mode cutover: runtime_v2 uses pty_mode only, daemon inherits compose tty by default, explicit override precedence and invalid-mode tests added; API bridge now uses pty_mode semantics and rejects legacy pty field.

title: Compose tty: attach/session PTY default parity
description: Ensure daemon-managed execution attach/session streams apply the same compose tty default semantics as create_execution (inherit from compose service tty unless explicitly overridden by caller/session mode). Remove any remaining legacy PTY fallback behavior in attach/session handling.
acceptance_criteria: 1) attach/session path resolves inherit PTY from compose tty metadata consistently with create_execution. 2) explicit caller/session PTY mode overrides inherited default. 3) tests cover tty=true, tty=false, and override precedence for attach/session flow.
notes: Progress dependency landed via vz-yc4i.1: exec output attach path now subscribes daemon session registry (StreamExecOutput) instead of unimplemented stub. Remaining: enforce/verify PTY default + override parity semantics for attach/session control path once backend interactive session wiring (vz-yc4i.2) is in place.

title: Extract runtime_v2 into dedicated vz-runtime-proto crate
description:
Create a dedicated generated protobuf crate for Runtime V2 control-plane IPC (e.g. vz-runtime-proto). Move/duplicate runtime_v2.proto ownership out of vz-agent-proto so guest-agent vsock protocol and daemon IPC protocol are not coupled in one crate.

Scope:
- Add new crate with build.rs + generated code module for vz.runtime.v2 package.
- Keep vz-agent-proto focused on agent.v1 guest protocol.
- Update workspace deps and imports where runtime proto is consumed.
- Preserve checked-in generated code policy and GENERATE_PROTOS workflow consistency.

Acceptance:
- runtime_v2 generated types are imported from dedicated crate.
- vz-agent-proto no longer exposes runtime_v2 surface (or clearly marked transitional shim with deprecation).
- Cargo check passes for affected crates.
notes: Implemented dedicated Runtime V2 proto crate extraction. Added new crate crates/vz-runtime-proto (Cargo.toml, build.rs, proto/runtime_v2.proto, src/lib.rs, src/generated/vz.runtime.v2.rs) and moved runtime-specific proto-contract tests there. Updated workspace membership/dependencies in crates/Cargo.toml. Trimmed crates/vz-agent-proto to guest-agent protocol only: build.rs now compiles only proto/agent.proto, removed runtime module/re-export/tests from src/lib.rs, deleted proto/runtime_v2.proto and src/generated/vz.runtime.v2.rs from vz-agent-proto. Validation (scoped): cargo fmt/check/nextest for -p vz-agent-proto -p vz-runtime-proto passed (27 tests).
close_reason: Extracted Runtime V2 protobuf contract into new vz-runtime-proto crate; vz-agent-proto now owns only guest agent protocol. Workspace rewired and scoped fmt/check/nextest pass for affected crates.

title: Implement workspace manager abstraction above runtime backends
description:
runtime-v2/03-workspace-and-stack-upgrades.md and 06-backend-architecture.md define a workspace/runtime manager layer that sits between the stack control plane and the backend implementations, routing operations uniformly.

Currently StackExecutor talks directly to ContainerRuntime trait. There is no intermediate manager that:
- Routes to the correct backend based on capability/platform.
- Manages sandbox lifecycle as a first-class concern.
- Maps stack-level operations to sandbox+container primitives.
- Enforces that a Compose stack maps 1:1 to a single Sandbox.

Implementation:
- Introduce RuntimeManager struct above vz-runtime-contract::RuntimeBackend.
- RuntimeManager owns sandbox lifecycle (create, query, terminate).
- RuntimeManager dispatches container operations within sandbox scope.
- Migrate StackExecutor from direct ContainerRuntime calls to RuntimeManager.
- Drop compile-time backend aliasing in vz-cli in favor of runtime manager routing.
- Replace boot_shared_vm/create_container_in_stack with create_sandbox + create_container flow.

This is prerequisite for vz-l56 (remove transitional boot_shared_vm path) and vz-td9 (complete backend trait coverage).

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/03-workspace-and-stack-upgrades.md, 06-backend-architecture.md
close_reason: Closed

title: vz rm doesn't stop the VM — only marks state as terminated
description:
Running `vz rm <sandbox>` calls `cmd_terminate` which only updates the sandbox state in StateStore to 'Terminated'. It doesn't actually stop the running VM.

The VM continues consuming CPU/memory in the background. Need to:
1. Find the running VM for this sandbox (by sandbox_id)
2. Send shutdown signal / kill the VM process
3. Wait for cleanup
4. Then update state

Currently there's no way to map sandbox_id back to a running LinuxVm instance from the CLI.
notes: Additional progress: standalone CLI now persists per-sandbox VM snapshots on detach and restores on attach/continue, reducing legacy in-process VM leakage risk; cmd_terminate removes persisted snapshot artifact. Full closure still requires macOS e2e validation + daemon-owned instant sandbox lifecycle cutover.

title: E2E test cgroup cpu.max enforcement
description: Add runtime_e2e test: container with cpus=0.5, read /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.max inside container, verify quota=50000 period=100000.
close_reason: E2E test cgroup_cpu_max_enforcement added to runtime_e2e.rs. Creates container with cpus=0.5, reads /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu.max, asserts 50000 100000.

title: Stack runtime alignment: remove transitional boot_shared_vm/create_container_in_stack path
description:
Remove the transitional boot_shared_vm and create_container_in_stack methods from ContainerRuntime trait and StackExecutor.

runtime-v2/03-workspace-and-stack-upgrades.md mandates: a Compose stack maps 1:1 to a single Sandbox. Orchestrator calls create_sandbox with aggregated VirtioFS mounts and network config, then calls standard create_container for each service.

Current state: StackExecutor::execute() calls boot_shared_vm() to start a shared VM, then create_in_stack() for each service. These are transitional methods that bypass the Sandbox abstraction.

Implementation:
- Replace boot_shared_vm() with RuntimeManager::create_sandbox() (from vz-kn4).
- Replace create_in_stack() with standard create_container() within sandbox scope.
- Remove boot_shared_vm and create_container_in_stack from ContainerRuntime trait.
- Update all backend implementations (MacosRuntimeBackend, test mocks).
- Update StackExecutor to use RuntimeManager.

Blocked by vz-kn4 (workspace manager abstraction).

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/03-workspace-and-stack-upgrades.md
close_reason: Wave 4 implementation complete

title: Fix user LaunchAgent bootstrap label conflict
description: User-mode provisioning installed a LaunchAgent with label com.vz.guest-agent, which conflicted with the system-mode label and caused launchctl bootstrap to fail with Input/output error. Use a distinct user label and plist filename for user mode so user-session auto-start works without manual launchctl intervention.
notes: Implemented in vz-cli provision module: user mode now writes ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.vz.user-guest-agent.plist with label com.vz.user-guest-agent (distinct from system com.vz.guest-agent). Also fixed LaunchAgent ProgramArguments to use guest-visible path /Users/dev/Library/Application Support/vz/vz-guest-agent instead of host mount path. Rebuilt and self-signed vz binary, reprovisioned base-user-clean image, and validated automatic agent availability with vm exec whoami returning dev without manual launchctl bootstrap.
close_reason: User-mode LaunchAgent now autoloads successfully; end-to-end host-to-guest exec works without manual bootstrap.

title: Restart policy implementation

title: Stack executor: execute Actions via vz-oci

title: Sandbox VM is not persisted — vz -c cannot re-attach to a stopped VM
description:
After exiting a sandbox (shell exits), the VM process dies but the sandbox record stays in StateStore as 'Ready'. Running `vz -c` finds the record but there's no running VM to attach to — we'd need to re-boot the VM.

The current `attach_to_sandbox_by_id` function tries to boot a new VM and attach, but this means losing any state from the previous session (installed packages, running processes, etc).

Options:
1. Save VM state (checkpoint) on detach, restore on re-attach
2. Keep VM running in background after detach
3. Accept re-boot and document that state is ephemeral
4. Use save/restore for named sandboxes only

This is fundamental to the `vz -c` experience — without persistent VMs, 'continue' is misleading.
notes: Implemented snapshot persistence for standalone sandbox sessions: interactive detach now saves VM state to .vz-runtime/sandboxes/<sandbox_id>.state and stops VM; subsequent attach/continue restores from snapshot (with cold-boot fallback on restore failure). Remaining: macOS e2e validation to confirm state continuity guarantees and edge-case recovery behavior.

title: Validate macOS snapshot restore continuity for sandbox attach/continue
description: Run end-to-end validation on macOS for CLI snapshot-backed sandbox lifecycle: create sandbox -> install/change state -> detach -> vz -c / vz attach restore -> verify state persists; verify fallback on corrupt snapshot and rm cleanup semantics. Capture reproducible steps and update n6dc/ju1r closure notes with evidence.

description: Reorient vz CLI around sandboxes as primary abstraction. Wave 1: proto changes for PTY, guest agent PTY allocation, host-side methods, LinuxVm interactive exec, standalone sandbox boot, CLI default command, attach logic, ls/rm/inspect/attach commands. Wave 2: restructure CLI with image/stack/debug subgroups.

CLI now saves VM state snapshot on detach, restores snapshot on attach/continue (with cold-boot fallback), and cleans snapshot artifact on rm. This improves vz -c semantics while daemon/session-registry cutover remains pending.

title: Runtime V2 API: replace OpenAPI sandbox surface stubs with real handlers
description:
Replace the 501 NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs in vz-api with real handlers for /v1/sandboxes, /v1/containers, /v1/executions, /v1/checkpoints, /v1/leases, /v1/receipts.

Currently vz-api only has working handlers for /v1/capabilities and /v1/events. All other resource endpoints return 501.

Implementation (per runtime-v2/05-transport-contracts.md):
- /v1/sandboxes: CRUD + list, backed by Sandbox domain entity.
- /v1/containers: CRUD + list + logs, backed by observed_state + runtime.
- /v1/executions: create + stream + signal + resize, backed by Execution entity.
- /v1/checkpoints: create + restore + fork, backed by Checkpoint entity with capability gating.
- /v1/leases: open + heartbeat + close, backed by Lease entity.
- /v1/receipts: get by operation ID, backed by Receipt entity.

All handlers must:
- Accept Idempotency-Key header for mutating operations.
- Return stable machine error codes in error envelope.
- Include request_id in all responses.
- Enforce lease validity where required.

Blocked by vz-c0z (idempotency framework) and domain model entities (vz-arp, vz-k3l, vz-22d).

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/05-transport-contracts-grpc-openapi.md
notes: Sandbox CRUD handlers implemented (POST /v1/sandboxes, GET /v1/sandboxes, GET /v1/sandboxes/{id}, DELETE /v1/sandboxes/{id}). Still blocked by vz-c0z (idempotency keys) for full closure.
close_reason: Closed

title: BuildKit: ENOSPC in VM /var/lib/buildkit during multi-image external sweeps
description:
In the same 52-Dockerfile sweep, multiple builds failed with no space left on device errors inside the BuildKit VM snapshot store (/var/lib/buildkit/runc-overlayfs/snapshots).

Evidence:
- Results: /tmp/vz-github-build-sweep-results-v2.tsv
- Logs: /tmp/vz-github-build-sweep-logs-v2
- Representative failures include npm/apt/package extraction and layer writes failing with ENOSPC.

Observed behavior:
- build cache du/prune does not report reclaimable data in this state, while builds still fail with ENOSPC.

Impact:
- Prevents completion of large compatibility runs and biases results toward infra failure rather than Dockerfile compatibility.

Suggested direction:
- Expose reliable BuildKit disk usage reporting for VM-backed store.
- Provide a deterministic cleanup/reset path for BuildKit VM storage used by builds.
- Consider guardrails before build start when available space is below threshold.
acceptance_criteria: 1) A documented/CLI cleanup path can recover from ENOSPC without manual state surgery.\n2) Space reporting reflects actual BuildKit VM usage and correlates with ENOSPC conditions.\n3) A 50+ Dockerfile sweep no longer fails due to VM storage exhaustion under normal conditions.

title: Runtime exec control parity: implement resize/cancel primitives across transports
description:
Runtime contract includes `resize_exec_pty` and `cancel_exec`, but transport/backend surfaces currently provide only partial exec control coverage.

Required: add end-to-end support for resize and cancel semantics (gRPC, OpenAPI adapter, backend manager), including deterministic error behavior when unsupported by capability.

Acceptance:
- Resize and cancel requests are available through transport APIs.
- Backends enforce capability-gated behavior with stable `unsupported_operation` diagnostics where needed.
- Integration tests cover ordering/race behavior with streaming exec output.
close_reason: Closed

title: Runtime gRPC contract split: dedicated runtime proto crate + translation layer
description:
Establish daemon IPC contract layering that mirrors proven pattern from gpu-cli-two: (1) generated protobuf contract crate for runtime control-plane RPCs only, and (2) explicit translation layer between proto messages and runtime-domain types.

Scope:
- Either extract runtime_v2.proto from vz-agent-proto into a dedicated crate (e.g. vz-runtime-proto) or keep source file but expose runtime-specific crate boundary.
- Add translation module/crate for proto<->vz-runtime-contract conversions (no handler-local ad hoc mapping).
- Keep vz-agent-proto focused on guest-agent/vsock protocol; avoid mixing daemon IPC and guest protocol concerns in one surface.
- Add compile-time and round-trip tests for conversion coverage across all Runtime V2 entities.

Acceptance:
- vz-runtimed and daemon client use runtime proto crate for IPC contract.
- API/CLI adapters depend on translation layer, not generated types directly.

title: BuildKit VM boot intermittently fails with VZErrorDomain:2 during long sweep runs
description:
During extended external build sweeps, some builds fail immediately at VM boot with VZErrorDomain:2 indicating missing virtualization entitlement. In this run, failures were partially recoverable by re-signing and retrying.

Evidence:
- Results: /tmp/vz-github-build-sweep-results-v2.tsv (attempts column shows retry behavior)
- Logs: /tmp/vz-github-build-sweep-logs-v2

Observed behavior:
- Intermittent startup failures with com.apple.security.virtualization message.
- Retry after re-signing sometimes succeeds.

Impact:
- Causes false negatives in compatibility runs and unstable long-running build automation.

Suggested direction:
- Improve diagnostics around entitlement validation at startup (preflight check).
- Add optional auto-retry with explicit user-facing remediation when this error is hit.
- Ensure developer signing workflow is robust and verifiable for repeated build invocations.
acceptance_criteria: 1) VM boot preflight clearly reports entitlement state before attempting build VM startup.\n2) A deterministic remediation path exists and is surfaced in CLI output when VZErrorDomain:2 occurs.\n3) Long multi-build sweeps no longer exhibit intermittent entitlement-related boot failures.

title: Add secret volume type with read-only lifecycle-scoped semantics
description:
runtime-v2/01-domain-model.md defines Volume types: bind, named, ephemeral, secret. Secret volumes are read-only and lifecycle-scoped to the container.

Currently VolumeManager handles bind, named, and ephemeral but has no secret volume concept.

Implementation:
- Add Secret variant to MountSpec in spec.rs.
- Secret mounts are read-only (enforced at OCI spec generation in bundle.rs).
- Secret data is sourced from stack secrets config or file reference.
- Lifecycle-scoped: secret mount is removed when container is removed.
- Never persist secret content in state store (only reference/path).
- Add Compose mapping for Docker secrets syntax.

Reference: private-vz/runtime-v2/01-domain-model.md
close_reason: Wave 6 implementation complete

title: [vz-stack] Secrets with file-based secrets cause Virtualization.framework directory sharing errors
description:
## INVESTIGATION COMPLETE

### Two Issues Found:

#### Issue 1: Single-service with secrets (CRASH)
- **Status**: Fails with error
- **Error**: "Invalid virtual machine configuration. A directory sharing device configuration is invalid."
- **Root Cause**: In single-container (non-shared-VM) mode, when bind mount secrets are added via mount_specs_to_shared_dirs(), VZ validation fails

#### Issue 2: Multi-service with secrets (WRONG MOUNT)
- **Status**: Works but mounts incorrectly
- **Issue**: Secret file is mounted as a DIRECTORY instead of a FILE
- **Evidence**: 
  - Host file: ~/.vz/stacks/<name>/secrets/<name>/db_password (is a FILE)
  - Container: /run/secrets/db_password (is a DIRECTORY)

### Root Cause Analysis:

**Issue 1 (Single-service crash)**: Likely an issue in the single-container VM creation path when VirtioFS directory shares are configured. The VZ API rejects the configuration somehow when bind mounts are added.

**Issue 2 (Directory instead of file)**: The mount is pointing to the parent directory instead of the specific file. This is a path construction bug in secrets_to_mounts or how it's used.

### Workaround:
Use multi-service compose files (which use shared VM) as a workaround.

### Next Steps:
1. Debug VZ validation in single-container mode - what's invalid?
2. Fix mount path for secrets in shared VM mode

This migration should eliminate store-only mutation paths for side-effecting operations and provide production-grade control-plane behavior.

title: [vz-stack] UX: Add resource usage to 'vz stack ps' output
description:
The 'vz stack ps' output doesn't show resource usage, making it hard to see how much CPU/memory services are using.

## Current Output
```
SERVICE              STATUS         CONTAINER ID                            
--------------------------------------------------------------------------
api                  running        api                                     
web                  running        web                                     
db                   running (ready) db                                      
```

## Problems
- No port information visible
- No health status unless explicitly '(ready)'
- No resource usage shown
- Container ID is just the service name

## Proposed Wireframe

```
vz stack ps my-app

SERVICE   STATUS      HEALTH       PORTS                 CONTAINER    CPU/MEM
────────  ──────────  ──────────   ────────────────────  ──────────  ────────
api       ● running   ✓ healthy    3000:3000             api          2% / 64MB
web       ● running   -            8080:80 → 80          web          1% / 32MB
postgres  ● running   ✓ healthy    5432:5432 → 5432     postgres     3% / 128MB
redis     ◌ stopped   -            -                     -            -

Use 'vz stack logs my-app' for full logs
Use 'vz stack exec my-app <service>' to run commands
```

## Improvements Needed
- Clear status icon
- Health status with checkmark or X
- Full port mapping visible (host->container)
- Resource usage (CPU/MEM)
- Better visual hierarchy
close_reason: Wave 5: UX improvements

title: Stack Control Plane State Reconciliation Hardening
description:
Keep stack orchestration fully reconstructable and restart-safe by strengthening control-plane durability and recovery semantics while preserving current SQLite-based architecture.

## Success Criteria
- All control-plane state required for deterministic restart and resume is either persisted or explicitly reconstructed from immutable inputs.
- Reconcile resume points and checkpoint transitions are provable and resumable.
- Recovery workflows are validated with crash/partial-progress scenarios.
- Event cursor + API/CLI query semantics remain stable.

## Architecture
- Use SQLite as control-plane source of truth for desired/observed/reconcile/events with explicit control metadata and schema-version contracts.
- Treat runtime-only state as ephemeral and intentionally reconstructed only when safe.

## Plan Reference
- planning/stack-control-plane-state-reconciliation/README.md

title: Guest-exec mode ignores --volume mounts
description:
In guest-exec mode, `--volume /host:/guest:ro` results in "No such file or directory" because:

1. `run_rootfs()` (runtime.rs:1335) destructures `mounts: _` — never converts to SharedDirConfig
2. VM config is created without mount VirtioFS shares
3. initramfs only mounts `rootfs` and `oci-runtime-bin` tags, not `vz-mount-*`

Fix requires:
- Wire mounts through `mount_specs_to_shared_dirs()` in `run_rootfs()`
- Add dynamic VirtioFS mounting in initramfs for `vz-mount-*` tags
- Guest agent needs to mount VirtioFS shares before exec, or initramfs does it
close_reason: Fixed both issues: (1) pipe inheritance hang in process.rs by waiting for child exit before draining pipes, (2) guest-exec volume mounts by wiring mount_specs_to_shared_dirs() and adding initramfs support for vz.mount.* kernel cmdline params. E2E verified.
